rationale: I split the broad discipline into 13 practitioner-facing subspecialties to balance coverage, discoverability, and mutual exclusivity. Each card reflects how professionals introduce themselves (e.g., 'Solid Mechanics', 'Aeronautics & Astronautics', 'Propulsion') and maps to a coherent body of papers so a given article should fit one slot. I kept computational/experimental methods as separate subspecialties rather than subsuming them under application areas to help readers who follow methodological advances. I intentionally did not create a standalone 'Materials' card (goes to Materials Science) and avoided over-splitting propulsion/turbomachinery vs. thermal to prevent tiny audiences and confusing overlap.

subspecialties.name: Solid Mechanics
subspecialties.tagline: Stress, strain, fracture, plasticity, structural materials
covers: Continuum mechanics of solids, elasticity, plasticity
Fracture mechanics, failure analysis, contact mechanics, structural materials testing
explicitly_excludes: Materials Science (materials synthesis, microstructure-focused characterization)
Structural Engineering (large-scale civil/architectural structures)

subspecialties.name: Structural Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Design and analysis of civil and aerospace structures
covers: Beam/plate/shell design, structural reliability, bridge/building/aero-structure analysis
Codes, large-scale load-bearing systems, seismic and wind design
explicitly_excludes: Solid Mechanics (fundamental constitutive theory and small-scale material failure)
Aeronautics & Astronautics (aircraft-level systems and aerodynamics)

subspecialties.name: Fluid Mechanics
covers: Fundamental and applied studies of laminar/turbulent flow, boundary layers
Multiphase flows, hydrodynamics, flow instability and transition
explicitly_excludes: Aeronautics & Astronautics (aircraft flight dynamics and systems-level aerospace research)
Propulsion & Turbomachinery (engine-specific fluid systems)

subspecialties.name: Aeronautics & Astronautics
subspecialties.tagline: Aircraft and spacecraft systems, flight dynamics, guidance
covers: Aircraft/spacecraft design, flight dynamics, orbital mechanics, guidance/navigation
Aerodynamic design for aircraft and spacecraft, systems integration
explicitly_excludes: Propulsion & Turbomachinery (engines and powerplants)
Control theory papers with no clear aerospace application go to Dynamics, Vibrations & Control

subspecialties.name: Propulsion & Turbomachinery
subspecialties.tagline: Engines, rockets, gas turbines, combustion and turbomachinery
covers: Jet engines, rocket propulsion, gas turbines, combustion modeling and experiments
Turbomachinery aerodynamics, performance, and design optimization
explicitly_excludes: Thermal & Heat Transfer (pure heat transfer studies not tied to engines)
Chemical propulsion chemistry-heavy work (may route to Chemical Engineering)

subspecialties.name: Thermal & Heat Transfer
subspecialties.tagline: Conduction, convection, radiation, thermal systems and energy
covers: Conduction/convection/radiation theory, heat exchangers, thermal management
Phase-change heat transfer, microscale and nanoscale thermal transport
explicitly_excludes: Propulsion & Turbomachinery (engine-specific thermal studies)
Materials Science (thermophysical property measurement focused on new materials)

subspecialties.name: Dynamics, Vibrations & Control
subspecialties.tagline: Rigid-body dynamics, vibration, controls, estimation and sensors
covers: Multibody dynamics, vibration analysis, structural dynamics, modal analysis
Control systems for mechanical and aerospace platforms, state estimation, adaptive/robust control
explicitly_excludes: Robotics & Mechatronics (application of control to robot hardware belongs there)
Aeronautics & Astronautics (flight dynamics papers specific to aircraft)

subspecialties.name: Robotics & Mechatronics
subspecialties.tagline: Robots, actuators, sensors, embedded mechanical systems
covers: Robot design, manipulation, legged and mobile robots, actuation and sensing hardware
Mechatronic system integration, real-time control of mechanical devices
explicitly_excludes: Dynamics, Vibrations & Control (theory papers with no hardware focus)
Computer Science topics like purely algorithmic AI (go to CS categories)

subspecialties.name: Manufacturing & Additive Manufacturing
subspecialties.tagline: Production processes, machining, 3D printing, process optimization
covers: Casting, forging, machining, automation in manufacturing, additive manufacturing processes
Process modeling, tolerancing, quality control, production systems
explicitly_excludes: Materials Science (new material development and characterization)
Robotics & Mechatronics (robotic automation hardware belongs there)

subspecialties.name: Computational Mechanics
subspecialties.tagline: FEM, CFD, reduced-order models, numerical simulation methods
covers: Finite element/volume methods, numerical solvers, mesh generation, model reduction
High-performance simulation workflows and verification/validation studies
explicitly_excludes: Applied CFD/fluid-mechanics papers where the main contribution is physics (go to Fluid Mechanics)
Machine learning algorithm work with no mechanics application (go to ML/CS categories)

subspecialties.name: Experimental Methods & Instrumentation
subspecialties.tagline: Measurement techniques, test rigs, sensors, experimental validation
covers: Experimental methods for mechanics/fluids, sensor design, wind tunnels, test apparatus
Data acquisition, uncertainty quantification, experimental validation protocols
explicitly_excludes: Papers whose main contribution is the physics discovery (route to the appropriate topic area)
Pure instrumentation for biology/medicine (may go to Biomedical engineering)

subspecialties.name: Vehicle & Automotive Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Automotive systems, vehicle dynamics, powertrain and NVH
covers: Vehicle dynamics, chassis design, powertrain development, NVH (noise, vibration, harshness)
Automotive systems integration, testing, and electrified vehicle technologies
explicitly_excludes: Aeronautics & Astronautics (air/space vehicles)
Propulsion & Turbomachinery (engine research not specific to vehicles)

subspecialties.name: Biomechanics & Medical Devices
subspecialties.tagline: Mechanics of biological systems and medical device design
covers: Tissue mechanics, implant design, prosthetics, device biomechanics, mechanobiology interfaces
Medical device testing, biomechanical modeling and simulation
explicitly_excludes: Biomedical research that is primarily biological/clinical (route to Biomedical Sciences)
Materials development for implants (go to Materials Science)

uncovered_concerns: Acoustics & Noise Control: considered as a separate card but many acoustics papers live within Dynamics, NVH, or Aero; decided to fold into Dynamics/Automotive for clarity.
Energy systems and HVAC: common cross-cutting topic split between Thermal & Heat Transfer and Manufacturing/Automotive depending on context rather than a standalone card.

Materials-focused mech papers: excluded because Materials Science handles materials-per-se; mechanics papers using materials characterization should pick Solid Mechanics or Structural Engineering depending on emphasis.
Pure ML for mechanics: tempting to create 'Machine Learning in ME' but methodological ML papers without a clear mechanical/aerospace application belong in CS/ML categories.
